title: PTD Today — Media

items.title: Would you go to an in-person dating app event?

ai.summary: Based on the available description, Gen Z — described as the online generation — is increasingly struggling with loneliness. Dating app Hinge announced a $1 million fund to support social groups in New York, Los Angeles and London to run free or affordable in-person events, as a global expansion of its One More Hour social impact initiative.
bullets: Gen Z facing rising loneliness despite being highly online.
Hinge created a $1 million fund for social groups.
Funding targets New York, Los Angeles and London.
Grants are to support free or affordable social events for young people.
This expands Hinge's One More Hour social impact program globally.
takeaways: Hinge is investing in localized, real-world programming in three major metro areas (NY, LA, London).
Infrastructure and service providers in those cities may see continued or shifting demand tied to platform-driven events.
Digital platforms continue to blend online services with in-person experiences, highlighting the need to track localized usage patterns.

items.title: Build Knowledge Graph-powered AI Agents For Your Use Case | Databases for AI

ai.summary: Based on the available description… This session demonstrates building a knowledge graph automatically from raw documents and connecting it to an AI agent so the agent can understand your specific business. The presenter emphasizes an end-to-end build with no graph expertise required.
bullets: General-purpose AI agents lack company-specific knowledge; knowledge graphs address that gap.
The session shows automatic construction of a knowledge graph from raw documents.
The knowledge graph is connected to an AI agent so it understands your business.
No prior graph theory or query expertise is required to follow the build.
A prior episode compares VectorDB vs GraphDB for generative AI agents (linked in description).
takeaways: Enables AI agents to use internal documents and business knowledge rather than relying only on generic models.
Automated graph construction reduces the need to hire specialized graph/database experts.
An end-to-end, no-expertise-required approach can accelerate adoption of domain-aware AI in operations and planning.

items.title: Leveraging tax credits and incentives for sustainability projects

ai.summary: Based on the available description, Randa Barsoum of PwC US highlights that energy incentives are only part of the tax picture for sustainability projects and that a broader set of credits and incentives can support those investments. She points to state and local abatements, location-based incentives, and R&D tax credits tied to innovation as examples of overlooked tax value.
bullets: Energy tax incentives are common but not the only opportunity.
State and local abatements can materially affect project economics.
Location-based incentives may influence siting and investment choices.
R&D tax credits can apply to innovation like reimagined packaging or processes.
Viewing sustainability through a broader tax lens can unlock meaningful value.
takeaways: Evaluate state and local abatements when deciding site location to improve project economics.
Factor location-based incentives into siting and expansion plans for grid or data center assets.
Assess R&D tax credits for operational or design innovations (processes, packaging, etc.) tied to sustainability.
Coordinate sustainability, tax, and operations teams to capture a wider set of incentives and maximize tax value.

items.title: Bringing In $251K A Year With 3 Jobs In The Bay Area

ai.summary: Based on the available description, Naseema McElroy, a 44-year-old labor and delivery nurse in Brentwood, California, brings in $251K a year through three jobs and runs a personal finance brand. She accumulated nearly $1 million in debt in 2015, started Financially Intentional to document her debt-payoff journey, and was debt-free by 2017. This profile is part of CNBC Make It's Millennial Money series.
bullets: Title notes she earns $251K annually from three jobs in the Bay Area.
Naseema McElroy is a labor and delivery nurse and founder of Financially Intentional.
In 2015 she had nearly $1 million in debt.
She launched Financially Intentional to document her debt-payoff journey.
She became debt-free by 2017; segment is part of CNBC Make It's Millennial Money series.
takeaways: Multiple income streams can materially increase annual revenue (three jobs → $251K).
Turning a personal financial journey into a brand can create alternative revenue channels.
Substantial debt can be paid off within a relatively short timeframe with disciplined strategy.
Profiles like this highlight individual financial resilience and storytelling as a business asset.

items.title: GeForce Garage - Summer of RTX Community Build

ai.summary: Based on the available description, NVIDIA’s GeForce Garage video highlights the winning Summer of RTX r/buildapc community build by u/JeffersonPutnam: a small-form-factor GeForce RTX 5090 rig. The build uses a Fractal Design Era 2 chassis and high-end components including an RTX 5090 Founders Edition, Ryzen 7 9800X3D, 32GB DDR5-6000, Corsair SF1000 PSU, and a Corsair Nautilus 240 RS AIO.
bullets: Winner: u/JeffersonPutnam’s SFF GeForce RTX 5090 build (Summer of RTX contest)
Chassis: Fractal Design Era 2 (small-form-factor)
GPU: GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition
Key components: Ryzen 7 9800X3D, 32GB Teamgroup T-Create DDR5-6000, Corsair SF1000, Corsair Nautilus 240 RS AIO
Full parts list available on PCPartPicker (link provided in description)
takeaways: Shows an RTX 5090 Founders Edition can be integrated into a small-form-factor Fractal Era 2 chassis using SFX power and AIO cooling.
The build pairs a high-end desktop GPU with a 1000W SFX (Corsair SF1000), highlighting significant power capacity needs for compact high-performance systems.
A 240mm AIO (Corsair Nautilus 240 RS) was chosen for cooling, indicating attention to thermal management in tight enclosures.
Complete parts list is provided (PCPartPicker) for direct replication or component-level review.

items.title: TTEC accelerates AI deployment and revenue streams with Google Cloud Consulting

ai.summary: Based on the available description… TTEC partnered with Google Cloud Consulting to rapidly prototype and deploy GenAI solutions, unlocking entirely new revenue streams while streamlining operations and boosting developer productivity. Google Cloud Consulting helped compress TTEC's digital transformation timeline and expand the ambitions of what’s possible for the business.
bullets: TTEC leveraged GenAI and Google Cloud infrastructure for rapid prototyping and deployment.
Partnership with Google Cloud Consulting accelerated digital transformation timelines.
AI solutions unlocked new revenue streams for TTEC.
Operations were streamlined and developer productivity improved.
Engagement expanded the company’s strategic ambitions.
takeaways: Engage cloud consulting to shorten AI deployment timelines and accelerate time-to-value.
Use robust cloud infrastructure to boost developer productivity and operational efficiency.
Prototyping GenAI can open new revenue opportunities worth prioritizing in planning.
Partnering with experts can expand strategic horizons without overburdening internal teams.
